Text: Adjusted age contains N=303 "unknown missing" values that are also missing 'Date of Administration'.
Variable names for tabulated MRI data (particularly XCP-D outputs) can be upwards of 167 characters, which may exceed variable name limits in some software and lead to truncation or import errors.
N=14 participants in `sed_basic_demographics` have a Maternal Age at V01 of 0; exclude these values from analyses until corrected.
A derived/rolled up substance use flag for Stimulants will be added based on positive instrument-specific Stimulant results.
Remove date taken, age, and language fields (computed based on *date coded*) to exclude from patch 2.1 *[BR data only]*.
Change coding for "Does not apply" to 8 to match the IBQ-R.

APA Level 2 was sometimes administered despite unmet gating criteria (e.g., missing Level 1 responses). These cases are not scored (“No additional inquiry required”) even when Level 2 responses are present. Level 2 item data will be removed to avoid confusion.
PNR data were incorrectly reported using TLFB versions 1/2 and will be updated to [version 3 specific to PNR](https://docs.hbcdstudy.org/latest/instruments/pregexp/su/tlfb/#v3).
Chronological and adjusted age fall outside of 3-9 months in N=74 V03 sessions (site entry errors); exclude age values prior to analysis.
Add item-level scores.
Participants missing all item responses are incorrectly scored as `0`; set values to null prior to analysis.

N=3 V04 session derivatives are missing corresponding tabulated data for FACE/MMN tasks. See [HBCD Private Release Notes](https://hbcd-docs-private.lassoinformatics.com/#download) for impacted participant IDs.

Add scores to `ph_cg_pms__pags`. Until added, scores can be calculated by following the [Scoring Procedures](https://docs.hbcdstudy.org/latest/instruments/physhealth/ecpromis-pags/#scoring) documentation.
Add sex-specific birth weight to `ph_ch_anthro` (see [Sex-Specific Birthweight for GA](https://docs.hbcdstudy.org/latest/instruments/physhealth/growth/#warning)).
The field for the date when PNV was stopped (`pex_bm_healthv2_preg__exp__pnv_007__01`) is blank, despite participants having reported stopping.
Note that items about aspirin use (`pex_bm_healthv2_preg__exp__pnv_{011|012}`) are largely blank.

Missing instrument fields for Sibling cohorts will be populated and *FamilyID* will be added to help identify siblings - [see details](https://docs.hbcdstudy.org/latest/instruments/demo/visitinfo/#warning).
N=4 V06 participants with <3 item responses are incorrectly scored as `0`; set values to null prior to analysis.

The `run-{X}` field may not reflect chronological acquisition order. While this affects both **raw BIDS and derivatives**, data remain internally consistent (i.e. run IDs match between raw and processed datasets).
Notes appear in the score field in both versions (Infant/Toddlerhood) and will be moved to a separate field.
Pro-rated scoring for `mh_cg_mapdb__tod` not yet implemented; N=16 participants missing scores.
The Data Dictionary element `type_data` for `average_bmi` will be corrected to `double` (currently=`character`).
Add source DICOMs for all imaging modalities.

The Demographics domain includes 2 tables with derived information grouped into visit-specific data ([Visit Info](https://docs.hbcdstudy.org/latest/instruments/demo/visitinfo/)) and general demographics ([Basic Demographics](https://docs.hbcdstudy.org/latest/instruments/demo/basicdemo/)). In a future release, these tables will be restructured to instead organize variables as either longitudinal (dynamic measures that change over time) or global (static measures, such as sex assigned at birth and race/ethnicity).
Nail type is `4` (Unknown) in the main results table (`*_nails_results`) and should be obtained from the specimen table (`*_nails_type`).

ICD codes for the `pex_bm_health*` instrument tables are inconsistently provided, sometimes missing corresponding names/labels. For example, medication names are present for the _Health V1- Medications_, while the _Health V2- Pregnancy_ instrument only has medication codes without corresponding labels. Until resolved, users can use external packages to merge ICD labels if needed: [Stata](https://www.stata.com/features/overview/icd/), [SAS](https://hcup-us.ahrq.gov/toolssoftware/ccsr/dxccsr.jsp), [R](https://www.rdocumentation.org/packages/icd/versions/3.3)
The 'instruction' Data Dictionary element is currently blank.

Scanner information must currently be parsed from raw BIDS data (specifically the scans .tsv files), as described [here](https://docs.hbcdstudy.org/latest/help/faq/#faq-scanner-info). Future releases will include a dedicated 'MRI Info' table that summarizes scanner information across participants, similar to the ABCD study ([see details](https://docs.abcdstudy.org/latest/documentation/imaging/admin.html#mr_y_adm__info)).
Add all age and `date_taken` fields (currently excluded due to use of coding rather than visit dates).
N=12 V03 participants with <3 item responses are incorrectly scored as `0` in `mh_cg_pms__cc__inf`; set values to null prior to analysis.
N=4 participants with no item responses are incorrectly scored as `0`; set values to null prior to analysis.
Inconsistent scoring: (1) item responses present, but score is null (N=1); (2) all items null, but score is `0` (N≥3).

Growth (`ph_ch_anthro`) filter ranges will be updated to be visit-specific, as current ranges allow biologically implausible values (see [Range Checks](https://docs.hbcdstudy.org/latest/instruments/physhealth/growth/#warning)).
Add household roster fields capturing the sex of listed individuals (adult & child tables).
Add `ph_cg_pms__sleep`  summary scores

A small subset of participants have implausible `gestational_age` (V01 only) values  for one or more instrument. Until corrected, review GA distribution to exclude outliers from analysis (should be positive and generally < 45 weeks).
The currently included Sequence field is blank across all instruments and will be removed.
Total non-parental hours/week (`ncl_ch_mlds_arr_hr_wk`) includes implausible values due to data entry errors. Exclude values >168 hours from analysis.
Add more fields to `ph_ch_vs` (current release only includes completion status and overall screening results).
Add Infant Sleep (IS) sub-scale score to `ph_cg_bisq`.

For a subset of participants, ERICA Codes (N=17) or Reliability (N=2) form scores are inaccurate; corrected scores are available to DUC-authorized users via the [HBCD Private Release Notes](https://hbcd-docs-private.lassoinformatics.com/#download). Users should further exclude participants with values of 0 across all scores (N=23 Codes, 12 Reliability) prior to analysis.
